`

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 11, 1989


Sabina Gilbert
511 5th Street
Lincoln, CA 95648

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-89-298

Dear Ms. Gilbert:

This is in response to your request for guidance regarding
your filing obligations under the provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your question does not involve a
specific pending decision, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (Copy
enclosed).²

QUESTION

Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of
economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of
Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730?

CONCLUSION

In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you
may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. The
conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and
disclosure obligations of a consultant. Any financial interests
you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual
statement of economic interests by filing Form 730.


___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-89-298
Page 2

FACTS

You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter
"counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an
agreement dated February 11, 1988. As counsel you provided legal
advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the
general plan. You expected your duties to consist of telephone
consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's
negotiating such documents. However, during the course of your
duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a
developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. You
have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those
negotiations.

You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721,
disclosing your economic interests. By telephone we have advised
you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might
be required to file Form 730 instead.

ANALYSIS

Section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified
elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those
positions, must file statements of economic interests. Those
officials must file Form 721. The city positions covered by
Section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief
administrative officer and city council members.

Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a
conflict of interest code. In its conflict of interest code, each
agency must designate those positions that involve the making of,
or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any
financial interest. (Section 87302.) Both the definitions of
"designated employee" and "public official" in the Act include any
consultants to an agency who make or participate in such agency
decisions.³ (Sections 82019 and 82048.)

_______________

3 A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice,
recommendation or counsel to a state or local government
agency....." (Regulation 18700(A)(2).) This term does not
include, however, a person who:

(A) Conducts research and arrives at
conclusions with respect to his or her rendition of
information, advice, recommendation or counsel
independent of the control and direction of the
agency or of any agency official, other than normal
contract monitoring; and

(B) Possesses no authority with respect to
any agency decision beyond the rendition of

Our File No. I-89-298
Page 3


A consultant is not included in Section 87200 as one of the
persons required to file a statement of economic interests
pursuant to that section. Accordingly, a consultant, such as
yourself, would file a statement of economic interests pursuant to
a conflict of interest code adopted by the city. We do not have a
copy of the conflict of interest code adopted by the city. You
should check that conflict of interest code to determine your
filing and disclosure obligations. However, in general, all city
public officials listed in the conflict of interest code, except
those specified in Section 87200, would file Form 730 with the
city as their annual statement of economic interests.

Please note that there are some differences in the
information that would be disclosed on Form 721 and Form 730. For
example, Form 730 requires disclosure of "business positions" on
Schedule C, but no such disclosure is required on Form 721. Also,
as discussed above, the extent of disclosure required by
consultants and other designated employees under a conflict of
interest code may be more limited than the disclosure required on
Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jeevan S. Ahuja
Counsel, Legal Division

KED/JSA/aa
Enclosures

(Continue footnote #3)
information, advice, recommendation or counsel.

Regulation 18700(a)(2)(A)-(B).

When an attorney is hired under a contract to perform legal
services for a specific matter (e.g., a particular piece of
litigation), he or she ordinarily comes within this exemption.
Essentially, the attorney is being called upon to deliver a
finished product and not to participate in or advise a city on
general governmental decisions requiring legal expertise. On the
other hand, when an attorney provides general advice and counsel
to the city on a continuing basis, it is the commission's view
that the attorney must file the appropriate statements of economic
interests. (See, e.g., Frederick Advice Letter, No. I-86-047;
Albuquerque Advice Letter, No. A-85-244; copies enclosed.)

CITY OF LINCOLN

OFFICE- Special Legal
Counsel

[SEAL: CITY OF LINCOLN
PLACER COUNTY, CALIF.]
AH '89

TELEPHONE 645-3314
511 5TH STREET - LINCOLN, CALIFORNIA 95648

May 1, 1989


Filing Officer
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Filing Officer:

     Enclosed are two Form 721's that I am submitting in my capacity as Special Counsel to the City of Lincoln. One is an assuming office statement and the other is the financial interest disclosure statement for 1988. I ask that you accept these statements and waive any late fines for the reasons set out below.

     I was retained by the City of Lincoln as Special Counsel by agreement dated February 11, 1988. As Special Counsel I was asked to provide legal advice to the City concerning the preparation of an amendment to the City's General Plan. The City Attorney was disqualified from advising the City on this project due to a conflict of interest.

     I served as City Attorney for the City of Lincoln from 1981 to 1983. Based on that prior experience, I expected my role as Special Counsel to involve limited contacts with the staff and Council. I expected to be consulted primarily by telephone and to be asked to prepare documents (such as agreements, ordinances, and resolutions) after the substance of each had been negotiated or otherwise worked out by staff. As such, I never thought of the need to file Form 721.

     My work for the City during the first few months under the agreement consisted of attending one meeting to discuss the scope of the project. On or about May 1988 I was asked to prepare a developer reimbursement agreement. After discussing the basic provisions with the City Administrator over the telephone, I prepared a draft and returned it, expecting only minimal changes. In fact, however, I soon became deeply involved in protracted negotiations over this agreement which lasted through the end of 1988. Since the conclusion of those negotiations I have done virtually nothing.

Filing Officer
Fair Political Practices
Commission
Page 2
May 1, 1989


Late in the year I started to reconsider the need to file
Form 721, because the work I was doing was beginning to look more
and more like that of a "consultant" within the meaning of the
law. Early in 1989 I discussed my situation with one of your
staff attorneys, Margaret Ellison. With her input and after
reviewing a number of advice letters on this issue which she
provided me, I have concluded that my status as Special Counsel
cannot be definitively categorized one way or another. Rather
than continue researching and wondering about the question, I
have opted to simply file the disclosure statements, and have
used the date of my agreement with the City of Lincoln as the
date of assuming office.

Please accept the enclosed forms for filing and waive any
late fines. If you wish to discuss this with me you may reach me
at (916) 624-3351 or (916) 428-1815.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Sabina D. Gilbert
Special Legal Counsel

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 23, 1989


Sabina D. Gilbert
Special Legal Counsel
511 - 5th Street
Lincoln, CA  95648

Re:  Letter No. 89-298

Dear Ms. Gilbert:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for information assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

Dear Filing Officer:

     Enclosed are two Form 721's that I am submitting in my
capacity as Special Counsel to the City of Lincoln. One is an
assuming office statement and the other is the financial interest
disclosure statement for 1988. I ask that you accept these
statements and waive any late fines for the reasons set out
below.

     I was retained by the City of Lincoln as Special Counsel by
agreement dated February 11, 1988. As Special Counsel I was
asked to provide legal advice to the City concerning the
preparation of an amendment to the City's General Plan. The City
Attorney was disqualified from advising the City on this project
due to a conflict of interest.

     I served as City Attorney for the City of Lincoln from 1981
to 1983. Based on that prior experience, I expected my role as
Special Counsel to involve limited contacts with the staff and
Council. I expected to be consulted primarily by telephone and
to be asked to prepare documents (such as agreements, ordinances,
and resolutions) after the substance of each had been negotiated
of otherwise worked out by staff. As such, I never thought of

In fact, however, I soon became deeply involved in protracted
negotiations over this agreement which lasted through the end of
1988. Since the conclusion of those negotiations I have done
virtually nothing.

Filing Officer
Fair Political Practices
Commission
Page 2
May 1, 1989


Late in the year I started to reconsider the need to file
Form 721, because the work I was doing was beginning to look more
and more like that of a "consultant" within the meaning of the
law. Early in 1989 I discussed my situation with one of your
staff attorneys, Margaret Ellison. With her input and after

at (916) 624-3351 or (916) 428-1815.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Sabina D. Gilbert
Special Legal Counsel

sections.question:
Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of 
economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of 
Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730?
sections.conclusion:
In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you 
may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. 
The 
conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and 
disclosure obligations of a consultant. 
Any financial interests 
you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual 
statement of economic interests by filing Form 730. 
, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) • )

Our Page 2
sections.facts:
You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter 
"counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an 
agreement dated February 11, 1988. 
As counsel you provided legal 
advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the 
general plan. 
You expected your duties to consist of telephone 
consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's 
negotiating such documents. 
However, during the course of your 
duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a 
developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. 
You 
have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those 
negotiations. 
You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721, 
disclosing your economic interests. 
By telephone we have advised 
you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might 
be required to file Form 730 instead.
sections.analysis:
section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified 
elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those 
positions, must file statements of economic interests. 
Those 
officials must file Form 721. 
The city positions covered by 
section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief 
administrative officer and city council members. 
Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a 
conflict of interest code. 
In its conflict of interest code, each 
agency must designate those positions that involve the making of, 
or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any 
financial interest. 
(Section 87302.) 
Both the definitions of 
"designated employee" and "public official" in the Act include any 
consultants to an agency who make or participate in such agency 
decisions. 3 
(Sections 82019 and 82048.) 
3 
A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any 
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, 
recommendation or counsel to a state or local government 
agency .... 11 
(Regulation 18700(A) (2).) 
This term does not 
include, however, a person who: 
(A) 
Conducts research and arrives at 
conclusions with respect to his or her rendition of 
information, advice, recommendation or counsel 
independent of the control and direction of the 
agency or of any agency official, other than normal 
contract monitoring; and 
(B) 
Possesses no authority with respect to 
any agency decision beyond the rendition of 


Our Page 3 
A consultant is not included in Section 87200 as one of the 
persons required to file a statement of economic interests 
pursuant to that section. 
Accordingly, a consultant, such as 
yourself, would file a statement of economic interests pursuant to 
a conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
We do not have a 
copy of the conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
You 
should check that conflict of interest code to determine your 
filing and disclosure obligations. 
However, in general, all city 
public officials listed in the conflict of interest code, except 
those specified in Section 87200, would file Form 730 with the 
city as their annual statement of economic interests. 
Please note that there are some differences in the 
information that would be disclosed on Form 721 and Form 730. 
For 
example, Form 730 requires disclosure of "business positions" on 
Schedule G, but no such disclosure is required on Form 721. 
Also, 
as discussed above, the extent of disclosure required by 
consultants and other designated employees under a conflict of 
interest code may be more limited than the disclosure required on 
Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. 
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you 
requested.

The

, et seq.

embedding.first_500_words: ` California Fair Political Practices Commission September 11, 1989 Sabina Gilbert 511 5th Street Lincoln, CA 95648 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-89-298 Dear Ms. Gilbert: This is in response to your request for guidance regarding your filing obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your question does not involve a specific pending decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (Copy enclosed).² QUESTION Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730? CONCLUSION In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. The conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and disclosure obligations of a consultant. Any financial interests you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual statement of economic interests by filing Form 730. ___________________________ ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. ² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660 Our File No. I-89-298 Page 2 FACTS You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter "counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an agreement dated February 11, 1988. As counsel you provided legal advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the general plan. You expected your duties to consist of telephone consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's negotiating such documents. However, during the course of your duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. You have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those negotiations. You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721, disclosing your economic interests. By telephone we have advised you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might be required to file Form 730 instead. ANALYSIS Section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those positions, must file statements of economic interests. Those officials must file Form 721. The city positions covered by Section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief administrative officer and city council members. Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a conflict of interest code. In its conflict of interest code, each agency must designate those positions that involve the making of, or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any financial interest.
